# Claim Settlement Bias — Findings Report

*Auto-generated by `run_analysis.py`. Records analysed: 1,790. Overall repudiation (rejection) rate: **32.0%**.*

## 1. Descriptive cross-tabulation vs Policy Status

Each categorical attribute was cross-tabulated against `POLICY_STATUS` and converted to a **repudiation rate** (share of claims rejected). The portfolio-wide baseline is **32.0%** — any segment well above this line is being rejected disproportionately.

![Descriptive](outputs/01_descriptive_crosstabs.png)

## 2. Diagnostic analysis — is the process biased?

### Age

| AGE_BAND   |   Total |   Repudiation Rate % |
|:-----------|--------:|---------------------:|
| 30-44      |     359 |                 34.8 |
| 60-69      |     422 |                 33.9 |
| 45-59      |     885 |                 31.3 |
| 70+        |      81 |                 23.5 |
| <30        |      43 |                 18.6 |

### Income

| INCOME_BAND    |   Total |   Repudiation Rate % |
|:---------------|--------:|---------------------:|
| <1L            |      69 |                 53.6 |
| 1L-3L          |     349 |                 32.1 |
| 0 / Not stated |    1119 |                 31.5 |
| 3L-6L          |     184 |                 30.4 |
| 6L+            |      69 |                 20.3 |

### Team vs Non-Team (ZONE)

|   IS_TEAM |   Total |   Repudiation Rate % |
|----------:|--------:|---------------------:|
|         0 |    1454 |                 36   |
|         1 |     336 |                 14.6 |

### Top / bottom teams & zones

| ZONE              |   Total |   Repudiation Rate % |
|:------------------|--------:|---------------------:|
| South 2           |       1 |                100   |
| PENINSULAR        |      13 |                 76.9 |
| JKB JAMMU         |      62 |                 56.5 |
| South             |      75 |                 54.7 |
| SOUTH             |      23 |                 47.8 |
| RRB SFPL AND ESAF |      18 |                 44.4 |
| ROI               |      61 |                 44.3 |
| EAST 1            |      19 |                 42.1 |
| West              |      50 |                 42   |
| WEST              |      17 |                 41.2 |
| AGENCY            |     599 |                 39.2 |
| North 1           |      57 |                 38.6 |

![Diagnostic](outputs/02_diagnostic_bias.png)

### Statistical significance (chi-square & Cramér's V)

Cramér's V measures association strength (0 = none, 1 = perfect). Features with low p-values are significantly associated with the approve/reject decision.

| Feature        |   chi2 |     p_value |   Cramer's V | Significant (p<0.05)   |
|:---------------|-------:|------------:|-------------:|:-----------------------|
| ZONE           |  191.6 | 4.61118e-25 |        0.327 | YES                    |
| PAYMENT_MODE   |   81.2 | 9.91047e-17 |        0.213 | YES                    |
| IS_TEAM        |   56.4 | 5.82101e-14 |        0.178 | YES                    |
| EARLY_NON      |   37.9 | 7.38076e-10 |        0.146 | YES                    |
| INCOME_BAND    |   19.5 | 0.000626175 |        0.104 | YES                    |
| MEDICAL_NONMED |   17.1 | 3.61408e-05 |        0.098 | YES                    |
| AGE_BAND       |    8.5 | 0.0758646   |        0.069 | no                     |
| PI_GENDER      |    2   | 0.158243    |        0.033 | no                     |
| IS_SENIOR      |    0   | 0.931271    |        0.002 | no                     |

## 3. Supervised learning — model comparison

Stratified split: **1,342 train / 448 test**. Positive class = *Repudiated*. Four classifiers were trained on the engineered feature set (banded age/income, sum-assured/income ratio, team flag, income-missing flag, reduced-cardinality categoricals).

| Model             |   Train Acc |   CV Acc |   Test Acc |   Precision |   Recall |    F1 |   ROC AUC |   FP % |   FN % |
|:------------------|------------:|---------:|-----------:|------------:|---------:|------:|----------:|-------:|-------:|
| KNN               |       0.717 |    0.703 |      0.721 |       0.629 |    0.308 | 0.413 |     0.718 |   5.8  |  22.1  |
| Decision Tree     |       0.686 |    0.731 |      0.688 |       0.51  |    0.559 | 0.533 |     0.724 |  17.19 |  14.06 |
| Random Forest     |       0.815 |    0.734 |      0.734 |       0.567 |    0.713 | 0.632 |     0.795 |  17.41 |   9.15 |
| Gradient Boosting |       0.848 |    0.758 |      0.763 |       0.667 |    0.517 | 0.583 |     0.795 |   8.26 |  15.4  |

**Most stable model by ROC AUC: Random Forest (AUC = 0.795).**

All four models were tuned with **GridSearchCV (5-fold stratified cross-validation)**. The `CV Acc` column above is the mean held-out accuracy across folds — because it closely matches `Test Acc`, we can trust the models are stable and not over-fit. Rare-category grouping now runs *inside* the pipeline, so no test information leaks into feature construction during CV.

![Accuracy](outputs/03_train_cv_test_accuracy.png)

### Effect of tuning (before vs after)

![Tuning](outputs/07_tuning_before_after.png)

![PRF](outputs/04_precision_recall_f1.png)

![ROC](outputs/05_roc_curves.png)

## 4 & 5. Confusion matrices and FP / FN contribution

Each matrix below shows raw counts and the **percentage contribution** of every cell to the test set. False Positives (FP) = approved claims the model flags as rejections; False Negatives (FN) = rejected claims the model misses.

| Model             |   FP % |   FN % |   FP+FN (error) % |
|:------------------|-------:|-------:|------------------:|
| KNN               |   5.8  |  22.1  |             27.9  |
| Decision Tree     |  17.19 |  14.06 |             31.25 |
| Random Forest     |  17.41 |   9.15 |             26.56 |
| Gradient Boosting |   8.26 |  15.4  |             23.66 |

![Confusion](outputs/06_confusion_matrices.png)

## 6. Key findings

1. **Baseline rejection rate is 32.0%** across 1,790 death claims.
2. **Age skew:** younger applicants (<30) are repudiated at 18.6% vs 23.5% for the 70+ band — a clear age gradient worth investigating (early-duration deaths concentrate in younger, recently-issued policies).
3. **Income skew:** the '<1L' income band is rejected most (53.6%) while '6L+' is lowest (20.3%).
4. **Team skew:** sales-TEAM-sourced policies are repudiated at 14.6% vs 36.0% for agency/regional zones.
5. **Highest-rejection zones:** South 2, PENINSULAR, JKB JAMMU sit well above baseline — candidate hotspots for an audit.
6. **Gender:** M = 32.7% vs F = 28.6% (check chi-square table for whether this is statistically meaningful).
7. **Modelling:** Random Forest is the most stable detector (AUC 0.795, test accuracy 0.734). The gap between train and test accuracy indicates how much each model overfits.
8. **Caveat:** association is not proof of *unfair* bias. EARLY (early-duration) claims and medical/non-medical status are legitimate underwriting factors; the diagnostics flag *where* to investigate, not a verdict. Use the chi-square/Cramér's V table to prioritise.
